name: Ensure credentials unused for 45 days or greater are disabled
body:
### Security intent

This control validates that **credentials unused for 45 days or greater are disabled** across AWS.

### Access condition reviewed

The assessment focuses on iAM users with credentials unused for 45 days or greater enabled. It reviews the identity, credential, role, or policy state that creates the access condition described by the control.

### Practitioner note

Identity findings deserve careful cleanup rather than blind deletion. Confirm ownership and business need, remove unnecessary standing access, and prefer federation, groups, roles, MFA, and short-lived credentials over direct long-lived grants.

This control maps to a benchmark-style requirement, so the expected state should match the benchmark wording unless your organization has an approved exception.
description: IAM users with credentials unused for 45 days or greater enabled
remediationDescription:
Perform the following to manage Unused Password (IAM user console access):

1. Login to the AWS Management Console:
2. Click `Services`
3. Click `IAM`
4. Click on `Users`
5. Click on `Security Credentials`
6. Select user whose `Console last sign-in` is greater than 45 days
7. Click `Security credentials`
8. In section `Sign-in credentials, Console password` click `Manage`
9. Under Console Access select `Disable`
10. Click `Apply`

Perform the following to deactivate Access Keys:

1. Login to the AWS Management Console:
2. Click `Services`
3. Click `IAM`
4. Click on `Users`
5. Click on `Security Credentials`
6. Select any access keys that are over 45 days old and that have been used and

- Click on `Make Inactive`

7. Select any access keys that are over 45 days old and that have not been used and

- Click the X to `Delete`

**References**

1. CCE-78900-8
2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html#remove-credentials
3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_finding-unused.html
4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_passwords_admin-change-user.html
5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_credentials_access-keys.html
